narrative.headline: What 2014 Kia Soul owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine problems are the dominant complaint pattern for this model, with owners describing sudden power loss and persistent check-engine lights that dealers repeatedly fail to resolve. Exterior lighting and electrical system faults — including at least one report of a seat catching fire — account for a significant share of remaining complaints. Powertrain issues round out the picture, with owners noting hesitation during gear shifts and sluggish acceleration.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the check-engine light activating unpredictably, accompanied by a severe reduction in power across the lower gears that persists even after multiple dealer visits. · 51 fire · 5 crash · 5 injured

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: Owners report difficulty unlocking electric doors at night, with the door-unlock system failing to respond promptly during urgent situations. · 1 fire

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report electrical faults serious enough to cause a heated seat to ignite, burning through the seat material. · 15 fire · 4 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle hesitating repeatedly when shifting gears and being slow to build speed, with at least one dealer recommending timing belt replacement. · 4 fire · 1 crash · 1 injured
